assumptions: The question concerns consolidated financial statements under IFRS.
The contemplated hedging relationship relates to foreign exchange risk associated with an intragroup balance or intragroup cash flow.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge as a top-level hedge accounting model for recognised assets or liabilities exposed to changes in fair value attributable to a particular risk.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge as a top-level hedge accounting model for exposure to variability in cash flows of recognised items or highly probable forecast transactions, including certain foreign currency risks.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation as a separate hedge accounting model for foreign currency exposure in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: No hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: The context states that only qualifying hedging relationships can apply hedge accounting, so a non-qualifying arrangement remains outside hedge accounting with gains and losses recognised under the normal IFRS 9 measurement basis.
